text:
Imagine this: you're about to embark on an exciting journey to one of the most captivating cities in the world—Copenhagen (哥本哈根) ! Now, you are preparing for this trip.

You need to list 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

To better evaluate the result, please organize your answers according to the following format:

Example:
	Location 1:
	location: UCLA
	reason: it's a famous university
	price: free
	opening days: all days
Location 2:
	...
Tips:
	1. Parking fee is high
	...
Amarienborg
Amarienborg
It's the home of the Danish royal family and a must-visit for anyone interested in royal history. You can witness the change of guard at 12:00.
It's the home of the Danish royal family and a must-visit for anyone interested in royal history. You can witness the change of guard at 12:00.
$1.25 (Reservation required)
$1.25 (Reservation required)

text: Frederiks Kirke
Frederiks Kirke
It's a famous modern architecture. It's also known as The Marble Church for its rococo architecture.

The Little Mermaid Statue
The Little Mermaid Statue
It's a bronze statue by Edvard Eriksen, depicting a mermaid becoming human. It's the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales.

The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. You may need a travel adapter.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. You may need a travel adapter.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euro

There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.

The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Pack your warmest clothes!

The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.

The “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” in Danish is “farvel”, “goodbye” in Danish is “tak”.
